title: C2 flag-surfacing reuses the existing client nav, result-frame catalogue, and the C1 Flag model — no A3 or C1 extension
context: TASK-020 / FGAP-008 (surface A3 flags to the client, mark the specific element each concerns, and offer accept / replace / request-catalogue-addition) raised three apparent forks: how a flag addresses its DOM element, how the client offers a catalogued replacement, and where a resolution persists. Each is settled by current source, not a user choice.
decision: Addressing: the client maps a flag to its row by reusing the existing prefill->row navigation (targetFormset / directRows / nestedFormset / stepChildFormset), parsing the flag's spec-prose `field` by the same index structure the apply functions already use — no `element_path` on FreetextFlag, no parser / A3 change. Catalogue: `build_catalogue_union(grounding)` rides the assist result frame (there is no client catalogue endpoint). Resolution maps onto the existing C1 Flag model: accept keeps the value (no row); replace substitutes the flagged span in the draft field (no row); request-catalogue-addition persists one Flag row (is_resolved=False) created in PlanCreateView.post after the plan PK exists — no `resolution_action` field, no C1 extension.
consequences: C2 touches only planner/views.py (result frame + create-view persistence), planner/static/wizard/js/ai-assist.js, and tests; FreetextFlag / the parsers (A3) and the Flag model / a migration (C1) are unchanged.
Request-catalogue-addition rows (is_resolved=False) are the durable admin signal; accept and replace are corrected in the draft content and leave no row.
Flag persistence is bound to plan creation, the only point a Plan PK exists; assist-time resolutions are client form-state until the create POST carries them.

references.label: result frame drops flags (criterion-1 change site)
client prefill->row navigation reused for flag addressing
C1 Flag model — the persistence target
plan PK created at the create POST
